priorities.action: PR outreach campaign for LongMemEval benchmark result (95.6%, #2 overall). 27 target blogs compiled. Pitch first: VentureBeat, Towards Data Science, MarkTechPost. Hook: only file-based memory in top tier, $0 infra, live production agent. Full target list in session context. Draft pitch email needed.

Build /sibyl-onchain `lp-collect-fees` action that compounds automatically: (1) call Uniswap V3 NonfungiblePositionManager.collect() on the SIBYL/cbBTC V3 position, (2) kyber-swap cbBTC → VIRTUAL with --exclude-pmm, (3) lp-add-v2 VIRTUAL + SIBYL to deepen primary LP. Converts daily fee drip into daily depth growth without manual intervention. Should be idempotent + rule-aware (respect rule 22/23 on both swap and LP steps). Reference implementation to live in scripts/lp-collect-fees.mjs. Doc under .claude/skills/sibyl-onchain/actions/lp-collect-fees.md.

Formalize JY partnership forward structure. First gesture already sent 2026-04-18: 3,000,000 SIBYL (0.3% supply, ~$5,400 at send time) to wallet 0xffb2b9ef…eb24, tx 0x0090635bc9b2dbfc2adf96d8033de09ecac2a72d252c4d75fcedbc66df54e736. Introduced Osmo + other high-value connectors. Operator to propose: (a) referral-tied future allocations for named new intros that close, (b) any deliverable expectations, (c) cadence. Entity at memory/entities/people/jy.json.

Prototype the next /sibyl-onchain action in tmp-test/. Candidates ranked by utility: (1) send-erc20 — most common, used in token matches and partnership gestures; (2) approve-token — composable building block; (3) wrap-eth / unwrap-weth — frequent; (4) lp-collect-fees — revenue-critical for cbBTC fee accumulation; (5) lp-mint-uniswap-v3 — higher complexity, useful for new LP positions. Follow the same pattern: sandbox in tmp-test/, live smoke test, harden, promote to scripts/, document under .claude/skills/sibyl-onchain/actions/, register in SKILL.md.

Add advisory dashboard check to CLAUDE.md Phase 2 session startup sequence so unread partner messages are surfaced at the start of every session, not discovered 2 days late. Proposal: add a new step to Phase 2 that iterates over every active partner in products/advisory-dashboard.json > active_partners and runs `doppler run -- node scripts/advisory-cli.mjs get-updates --project <id>`. Messages newer than last_session timestamp get surfaced immediately. This is an infrastructure edit to CLAUDE.md — requires explicit operator approval per the Infrastructure Rule. Present proposal + wait for yes/no.
Formalize Osmo partnership terms. Operator drafting structure. First gesture (2.24M SIBYL match) already sent, tx 0x7f612100…bdbf3. Entity at memory/entities/people/osmo.json.

ACP v2 migration: agent 18393 blocked by Privy wallet ownership. Operator sent question to Virtuals team. Awaiting response. CLI installed at /home/ubuntu/acp-cli, operator ENS wallet authed. If Virtuals can transfer ownership or provide non-browser auth, migration can complete.

Ask Bill for a thorough breakdown of WW3 game economics: revenue streams (game fees, ticket sales, War Chest %), token burn/emission, operational costs, hosting. Verify the game is at least slightly profitable for him and he is not bleeding money or tokens. Advisory duty: if the economics are upside-down, flag it and help fix before launch.

Plan domain migration: sibylcap.com → sibylcorp.org. Operator forming Sibyl Corp LLC (Delaware). sibylcorp.org to be registered. All subdomains (ping, partners, docs, blog, claim) migrate. X handle @sibylcap stays. Map every hardcoded sibylcap.com reference across codebase, x402 endpoints, Ping messages, agent bookmarks.
RULE: never hardcode live data in memory files. Fetch dynamically via MCP tools.
RULE: never request or handle advisory client private keys. deliver scripts, they configure their own keys.

RULE: when sharing files with the operator, use sibylcap.com/files/ page only (password-protected). Videos go to website/ root (served at /filename.mp4). Images go to website/images/. Always add entries to the VIDEOS or IMAGES array in website/files.html, then deploy website via vercel --prod.

RULE (Kyber PMM regression): Kyber routes through PMM providers (kipseli-prop, axima-v2, dexalot) reliably revert TRANSFER_FROM_FAILED at non-$1 sizes even with maxUint256 approval to the main router. On revert, retry the swap — Kyber's route selection is non-deterministic and fresh quotes often return DEX-only routes that succeed. See .claude/skills/sibyl-onchain/actions/kyber-swap.md 'Common failure modes' table.
